seo.title: Rollercoin Calculator | Earnings & Withdrawal Calculator
seo.description: Free Rollercoin earnings calculator. Calculate your daily, weekly, and monthly crypto mining rewards. BTC, ETH, DOGE, SOL and more.

about.title: About Us
about.p1: RollerCoin Calculator is an independent analytical tool developed to help RollerCoin players optimize their mining strategies.
about.f1: Earnings Calculator — View your daily, weekly, and monthly crypto earnings in USD.
about.f2: Withdrawal Timer — Find out exactly how long it takes to reach the minimum withdrawal limit.
about.f3: Power Simulator — Simulate how adding new miners or parts will affect your overall profitability.
about.f4: Live Market Data — Real-time exchange rates via Binance API for the most accurate results.
about.note: Note: This tool is independent and not affiliated with the official RollerCoin team. Data is based on the values entered; actual earnings may vary based on live game conditions.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: 1. Why are my earnings constantly changing?
faq.a1: Earnings in RollerCoin are not static. The 'Network Power' of your chosen coin changes second by second. When other players allocate their power to that coin or buy new miners, the total network power increases. Even if your personal power remains constant, your share of the pie decreases because the total pool got larger. Additionally, due to crypto market volatility, the USD equivalent of your earnings changes live.
faq.q2: 2. How do I know which league I am in?
faq.a2: You are assigned a league based on your total active 'League Power' in the game. When you enter your Username in our calculator to fetch data via the API, the tool automatically detects the exact league assigned to you by the game. If you are entering your power data manually, the calculator in 'Auto' mode places you in the approximately correct league based on your hash power sum. If it's incorrect, you can change your league manually from the dropdown menu.
faq.q3: 3. Which coin is the most profitable to mine?
faq.a3: There is no single correct answer for this because reward rates and the market price of coins (e.g., BTC, SOL, DOGE, etc.) change constantly. By looking at the USD column in our 'Earnings' table, you can see in seconds which coin offers the highest fiat return under the current network conditions. Remember, the highest USD yielding coin is generally the most logical choice, but you should also factor in the future potential price appreciation of that coin.
faq.q4: 4. How does the Withdraw Timer work?
faq.a4: This tool takes your current balance in the game and your real-time production (earning) rate to calculate exactly how many days and hours it will take for you to reach the minimum withdrawal limit for that specific coin. Since this calculation is based on current network power, an increase in network power might extend the withdrawal timeline.
faq.q5: 5. What is the Power Simulator used for?
faq.a5: If you are planning to spend your RLT or crypto to buy a new Miner or Rack, this tab allows you to simulate the purchase. When you enter the new hardware's Raw Power and Bonus Percentage, it adds it to your existing power and instantly shows what your new total power and new earnings will be. This helps you avoid making unprofitable miner purchases.
faq.q6: 6. How often is the data updated?
faq.a6: When you fetch data via the API (using your username or the 'Fetch Data' button), all league powers, block rewards, and block durations are pulled directly from the RollerCoin servers in real-time. Cryptocurrency prices are fetched from the Binance exchange API (with CoinGecko as a fallback) and reflect the live market rates at that exact moment. The data is cached locally for 5 minutes to avoid excessive API calls.
faq.q7: 7. Why does my league change automatically?
faq.a7: The calculator has an 'Auto League' detection feature. Based on your total qualifying power (Miners + Bonus + Rack power, excluding Games/Temp/Freon), it automatically places you in the correct league tier. If the auto-detection is incorrect or you want to explore a different league's earnings, you can disable 'Auto' mode and manually select any league from the dropdown menu.
faq.q8: 8. What is the difference between 'Current Power' and 'Max Power'?
faq.a8: 'Current Power' includes ALL power sources: Miners, Racks, Games (mini-game power), Temporary Power, Freon, and Bonuses. This is the total power used for block reward calculations. 'Max Power' (or League Power) only includes Miners + Racks + Bonus power and determines which league you belong to. Games, Temporary, and Freon power do NOT affect your league placement but do increase your mining earnings.
faq.q9: 9. Can I use this calculator on mobile devices?
faq.a9: Yes, the calculator is fully responsive and works on all modern mobile browsers including Chrome, Safari, and Firefox. The interface automatically adapts to smaller screens with touch-friendly controls. You can also install it as a Progressive Web App (PWA) on your home screen for quick access.
faq.q10: 10. What are League Charts and how do I use them?
faq.a10: League Charts show the historical trend of average network power and block payout for each league over time. You can select any league and cryptocurrency combination, then filter by date range (7 days, 30 days, or all time). This helps you identify patterns — for example, if a league's power is steadily increasing, your individual share of rewards will decrease proportionally. Use this data to decide which league to target or when to switch coins.
faq.q11: 11. How does the Merge Calculator work?
faq.a11: The Merge Calculator displays all available miner merge recipes from RollerCoin's crafting system. Each recipe shows the resulting miner, its power, bonus percentage, RLT cost, and required parts. You can set your Forge level (1-5) to apply the correct component discount, adjust merge count for bulk calculations, and set custom marketplace prices for components to get accurate total cost estimates. The tool helps you decide whether a merge is worth the investment before spending your RLT.
faq.q12: 12. What is a Progression Event and how does the event calculator help?
faq.a12: Progression Events are limited-time events in RollerCoin where you earn points by spending RLT (through marketplace trades or direct exchange) to unlock exclusive rewards at each level. Our event calculator shows all reward tiers, the RLT cost per level, multiplier calculations, and box opening costs. It helps you plan your budget before the event starts so you can decide how many levels you can afford and whether the rewards justify the investment.
faq.q13: 13. What cryptocurrencies does the calculator support?
faq.a13: The calculator supports all mineable cryptocurrencies in RollerCoin: Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Solana (SOL), Dogecoin (DOGE), BNB, Litecoin (LTC), XRP, Tron (TRX), and POL (Polygon/MATIC). It also calculates in-game token earnings for RLT, RST, and HMT. USDT earnings are shown but since it's a stablecoin, its price is fixed at $1. All coins except ALGO and USDT appear in the withdrawal timer since they can be withdrawn from the game.
faq.q14: 14. What is 'Block Duration' and why does it matter?
faq.a14: Block Duration (or Block Time) is the number of seconds between consecutive blocks being mined for each cryptocurrency. The default is approximately 600 seconds (10 minutes), but it varies by coin and can change over time. Shorter block times mean more blocks per hour, which means more frequent (but smaller) rewards. The calculator fetches current block durations from the API automatically, but you can switch to manual mode in Settings to override them.
faq.q15: 15. Why do some coin prices show differently than on exchanges?
faq.a15: Prices are fetched from Binance API by default (with CoinGecko as a fallback option). Minor differences from other exchanges are normal due to slight price variations across platforms. If Binance is blocked in your region, you can switch to CoinGecko in the Settings modal. The price is used only for the USD equivalent column — your actual crypto earnings remain the same regardless of which price source is selected.
faq.q16: 16. How do I install the calculator as a PWA (Progressive Web App)?
faq.a16: On Chrome (desktop or mobile), look for the install icon in the address bar or the 'Add to Home Screen' option in the browser menu. On Safari (iOS), tap the share button and select 'Add to Home Screen'. Once installed, the calculator opens like a native app, loads faster, and works even with limited connectivity since the service worker caches essential assets.
faq.q17: 17. How accurate is the calculator compared to actual in-game earnings?
faq.a17: The calculator provides a close estimate based on real-time data from RollerCoin's API. However, actual earnings can vary slightly because: (1) network power changes constantly as players join/leave, (2) block times fluctuate around the average, (3) there may be brief server-side maintenance periods. For the most accurate results, fetch fresh data frequently using the 'Refresh' button.
faq.q18: 18. What are Game Tokens (RLT, RST, HMT) and why are they shown separately?
faq.a18: Game tokens are in-game currencies with no external market price. RLT (RollerToken) is used for marketplace purchases, event participation, and miner crafting. RST is a secondary game token. HMT is used for specific in-game mechanics. Since they have no real-world USD value on exchanges, they are displayed in a separate tab from cryptocurrencies. Their block rewards and earning rates are still calculated using the same formula.
faq.q19: 19. What is Collection Bonus and how does it affect earnings?
faq.a19: Collection Bonus is a percentage multiplier applied to your total miner power based on the unique miners in your collection. Each distinct miner model you own adds its bonus percentage to a cumulative total. For example, if you have 5 unique miners with bonuses of 1%, 2%, 1.5%, 0.5%, and 3%, your total collection bonus is 8%. This 8% is applied to your raw miner power to give you 'free' extra hash power. The Power Simulator accounts for this when you add new miners.
faq.q20: 20. Does this site collect my personal data or RollerCoin credentials?
faq.a20: No. We do not have any database and do not store any personal information. Your RollerCoin username is only used to fetch publicly available profile data from the game's API — we never ask for or handle your password or login credentials. All calculations happen client-side in your browser. The only data stored locally (in your browser's LocalStorage) are your preferences like language, league selection, and cached calculation results.
faq.a21: Daily Bonus Quests are daily challenges in RollerCoin that offer additional rewards when completed. Our tool fetches and displays the current active quest including its description, required action, reward type, and Season XP bonus. You can also view quest history to track past quests and their rewards.

guides.title: RollerCoin Strategy Guides
guides.g1_p: If you are new to the RollerCoin world, your priority should be playing free mini-games regularly to keep your persistent power (PC level) at the maximum possible level. Game Strategy: Play around 40-50 games a day to upgrade your PC to Level 4. This ensures the hash power you gain from games lasts for a full 7 days. Focus on quick games like Cryptonoid. In the beginning, allocate 100% of your power to mining RLT so you can build a budget for miners later.
guides.g2_p: Big profits come from well-planned investments during Progression Events rather than daily raw mining. Tactic: Always keep a reserve of RLT in your account. When a Progression Event starts, flipping items on the marketplace or trading to gather event points will yield exclusive event miners for much lower costs.
guides.g3_p: The biggest mistake players make is buying miners with high raw power while ignoring Bonus Power. Every unique miner in your collection applies its bonus percentage to the raw power of your entire room. Example: If you have 100 Ph/s and 10% bonus, you get an extra 10 Ph/s for free. Bonuses only apply to unique miners in your collection.

support.title: Support & Referrals
support.p1: If you would like to support the development of this project, you can use the channels below. Every donation and referral support helps cover server costs and add new features.
support.disclaimer: None of the links, games, or Telegram bots shared here are our official sponsors. We have no developer affiliation with any of these projects. None of this is Financial Advice (NFA). These are just tools we personally use and find interesting. If you wish to support our site, you can register for these services using our referral links below.

desc.immutable: A leading Web3 gaming platform where you can discover, play, and earn in next-generation crypto games.

desc.freecash: Earn crypto, gift cards, and cash by playing games, completing surveys, and testing apps.
desc.rollercoin: If you visited this site and still haven't started playing RollerCoin (which is highly unlikely), we would appreciate it if you register using our referral link to support our site.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: RollerCoin Calculator respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your data.
privacy.s1_p: We do not collect personal data. There is no account creation, registration, or login on this site. We do not request or store your name, email address, or phone number.
privacy.s2_p: When you visit our site, technical data such as anonymized IP address, browser type, and visit time may be collected for analytics.
privacy.s3_p: Our site uses cookies through third-party services like Google Analytics and Google AdSense. You can manage cookies in your browser settings.
privacy.s4_p: We store your preferences (language, settings) in your browser's localStorage. This data remains only on your device.
privacy.s5_p: For any questions, you can visit the GitHub page.

charts.title: League Data Charts
charts.desc: View average power and payout trends for each league over the last 30 days.
charts.noData: No data available for this selection.
charts.seoContent: League Charts provide a historical overview of mining power and block payout trends for each league in RollerCoin. By analyzing these graphs, players can identify when network competition is increasing or decreasing, helping them make strategic decisions about which coins to mine. The chart plots two key metrics: Average Power (Gh/s) on the left axis showing total network hash rate over time, and Average Payout on the right axis showing block rewards in the selected cryptocurrency. Use the date range filters to zoom into specific time periods or view all-time data. This information is invaluable for long-term mining strategy optimization.

merge.title: Merge Recipes
merge.dragDropHint: No miners found matching your criteria.
merge.noResultsFound: No results found. Please enter a filter and search.
merge.partPricesDesc: Enter the current part prices from the marketplace to calculate merge costs more accurately. For parts left blank, API data will be used.
merge.seoContent: The Merge system (also known as Crafting) in RollerCoin allows players to combine mutation components — Hashboards, Fans, and Wires of various rarities — to create new miners or upgrade existing ones to higher levels. Each merge recipe has a fixed RLT cost plus component requirements. The Forge level (1-5) provides progressively larger discounts on component quantities, making it essential to upgrade your Forge before attempting expensive merges. Our Merge Calculator fetches all available recipes from the game and lets you calculate exact costs including component marketplace prices, helping you determine whether a merge is more cost-effective than buying the miner directly from the marketplace. Use the search and sort features to find the best value merges for your budget.

componentCalc.totalValueTooltip: Total Market Price - Total Forge Cost = Net Profit

seoArticle.title: What is the RollerCoin Profit Calculator?
seoArticle.p1: The core factor that instantly dictates your earnings within the RollerCoin ecosystem is the game's complex cryptographic math infrastructure. Our calculator determines this by looking at your power, total network power, and the live block reward using Live Market Prices down to the second via the Binance API.
seoArticle.p2: Our professional-grade calculator supports every cryptocurrency offered by the game, including Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Solana (SOL), Dogecoin (DOGE), BNB, Litecoin (LTC), XRP, Tron (TRX), and POL (Polygon). It also provides real-time per-block earnings for in-game tokens like RLT, RST, and HMT. The calculation engine accounts for each coin's block duration, league-specific multipliers, and network difficulty to deliver the highest possible accuracy.
seoArticle.li1: Live Market Prices: Instead of outdated static data, we pull live exchange rates from Binance for Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Doge, and more to show your true USD profits instantly.
seoArticle.li2: League System Integration: Fully simulates the hidden multiplier percentages added to your block pool distributions based on your active gaming league tier.
seoArticle.li3: Precision Withdrawal Timers: Enter your current coin balance, and our engine automatically structures exactly how many days and hours are left until you reach the minimum withdrawal threshold.
seoArticle.li4: Power Simulator: Planning to purchase a new miner or rack? Simulate the acquisition before committing your RLT to see exactly how much your earnings will increase. This prevents unprofitable hardware investments.
seoArticle.li5: League Charts & Trend Analysis: Track historical power and payout data for each league with interactive charts to analyze market trends and league popularity over time.
seoArticle.h_how: How Does the Earnings Calculation Work?
seoArticle.p3: The fundamental formula behind RollerCoin mining is straightforward: Your Earnings = (Your Power / Total League Power) × Block Reward. However, multiple complex variables lie behind this simple equation. Different block durations for each coin, different reward multipliers for each league, and constantly shifting network power make manual calculation nearly impossible. Our calculator automatically fetches all these variables from the API and presents your daily, weekly, and monthly earning estimates with USD equivalents in seconds.
seoArticle.p4: Additionally, the calculator shows the real-time league power distribution for each coin. This lets you see how competitive a coin is and how your rewards are split among other players. A less competitive coin in a high-power league could be more profitable than a popular coin in a lower league — the calculator helps you make such strategic decisions.
seoArticle.p5: Beyond earnings calculation, our platform offers a Merge Calculator that analyzes miner crafting recipes and an Event Calculator that helps you plan your Progression Event budget. The merge tool applies Forge-level discounts to component requirements and factors in marketplace prices for accurate total cost estimation. The event tool details rewards at each tier, required RLT amounts, and multiplier costs so you can plan your spending before the event begins.
seoArticle.p6: Conquering the leaderboards requires more than just basic math. Stay ahead by reading our encyclopedic deep-dives into Marketplace arbitrage opportunities, exact Collection Bonus configurations, and Free-to-Play persistence. Learn the strategies behind how experienced players accumulate dozens of Ph/s of power, how to start from zero as an F2P player, and how to deploy your RLT most efficiently during event seasons.
